# NATO Secretary General warns of Russia-China axis threatening transatlantic security

## Overview
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte delivered a speech at CEPA event highlighting growing Russia-China military cooperation as a direct challenge to European security and NATO unity. He urged stronger transatlantic bonds to counter hybrid threats from the axis.

## Details
Rutte cited joint Russia-China naval patrols in the Baltic Sea last month involving over 10 vessels. He referenced recent Chinese arms shipments to Russia valued at $2 billion since 2024. "This does not stand as just a European problem; it's a transatlantic imperative," Rutte stated. The speech coincided with NATO's reinforcement of Black Sea battlegroups. CEPA analysts noted 15% increase in Sino-Russian exercises in 2026. Event attended by 200 officials from US, UK, and EU states.

**Location**: Brussels, Belgium

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @cepa, which covers CEPA European security Russia China NATO transatlantic. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following CEPA European security Russia China NATO transatlantic will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
